Added docs/schemas/fixtures/async-task.terminal-failed.json — extended-naming boundary fixture for the W5 async-task schema covering the terminal-failed branch of the state machine. Why this matters: async-task.valid.json covers the awaiting-review happy path (3 completed steps, real result_plan_id, full evidence_ids) and async-task.invalid.json blasts 11 schema violations at once, but neither exercises the *valid-but-terminal* path that the failure_reason field exists for. terminal-failed.json fills that gap: state='failed' + failure_reason populated (Provider call timeout at step s2 after 30s) + steps array carries one completed/one failed/one stuck-pending step (the canonical terminal-step rollup pattern) + result_plan_id=null (because the task never reaches awaiting-review when it dies before assembly) + evidence_ids reflects only the steps that produced evidence. Added docs/schemas/async-task.schema.md — 202-line reader's guide for the W5 async-task envelope. Why this matters: the schema body itself (async-task.schema.json) is the single source of truth, but JSON Schema 2020-12 syntax doesn't carry the *why* — why is service_mode locked at creation, why is the step state enum narrower than TaskState, why is failure_reason capped at 500 chars. A downstream tool author or the W5 Day-0 C++ implementer hand-deriving AsyncTask.hxx would have to either re-derive every invariant from the spec + schema cross-walk or risk silently violating one.
